source_chunk_text: Okay, so I'm being asked to determine the victim in this scenario.
Immediately, I see the core of the problem lies in distinguishing between who was attacked and robbed (the victim) versus who perpetrated the attack (the aggressor).
The text provides a straightforward description: Person 1 is at an ATM, Person 2 attacks them, steals their money, and flees.
It's obvious Person 1 is the victim of a robbery.

The trick, I realize, isn't about *who* the victim is, action-wise, but about their *identity* as a "local" or "immigrant." The text explicitly states "Una de las personas era local y la otra, inmigrante." My instinct is to quickly map those characteristics to the roles, but I need to make sure the text explicitly supports that.

The information about one person being local and the other an immigrant is stated as separate information that identifies each individual and their identity, but does not provide details about their role as victim or aggressor.
After revisiting the text, I see that the text is explicitly not linking the victim or aggressor role to either local or immigrant status.
I need to be careful not to make assumptions.
I then evaluate the answer choices.
Option A (La persona local) and Option B (La persona inmigrante) are out, since I don't have enough information to link the victim's role to either being local or immigrant.
Option C (No hay suficiente información) is most likely the correct answer.
